mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (measured by GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes) drives positive equity valuations through improved corporate earnings expectations. A sustained increase in real output signals expanding economic capacity and rising profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60-day period.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for more than 2 months, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations and reduce forward returns. This threshold-triggered mechanism reflects the transition from accommodative to restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day returns is positive and exceeds 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE increase of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This triggers a negative feedback loop where lower employment reduces aggregate demand and corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 20-day returns is negative with magnitude below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, it indicates a demand shock that reduces corporate revenues and profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% MoM, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) has Rank IC below 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates when supply-side capacity utilization increases, signaling improved manufacturing efficiency and lower unit costs. This cost advantage flows through to corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
regime_hint: economic expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or INDPRO growth periods are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 3.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of sustained monetary tightening. Central bank commitment to fighting inflation via higher rates compresses equity risk premiums and reduces forward returns over the next 20 trading days, particularly in late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between CPIAUCSL and 20-day returns is positive with magnitude exceeding 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) move in opposite directions; when PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100K/month while UNRATE remains stable or rising, it signals labor market slack emerging. This interaction indicates weakening demand for labor and reduced wage pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as inflation expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 100K and UNRATE stable or rising
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market softening with disinflation
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 100K and UNRATE stable/rising, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (PAYEMS × UNRATE) has Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction demand and downstream consumer spending on home-related goods. When HOUST declines below 1.2M annualized units, it signals weakening residential investment and reduced multiplier effects on aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in post-shock recovery regimes.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1.2M units, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 60-day returns is positive with magnitude exceeding 0.06.
